questions.question: What is the primary function of a boot loader in an operating system?
options: A) To manage memory allocation
B) To manage file systems
C) To load operating system into memory
D) To manage peripherals
questions.explanation: A boot loader is a program that loads the operating system into memory when the computer starts up.

questions.question: What is the difference between a 32-bit operating system and a 64-bit operating system?
options: A) 32-bit OS can handle more memory
B) 64-bit OS can handle more processors
C) 64-bit OS can handle more memory and processors
D) 32-bit OS can handle more processors
questions.explanation: A 64-bit operating system can handle more memory and processors than a 32-bit operating system.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a process manager in an operating system?
options: A) To manage memory allocation
B) To manage file systems
C) To manage processes and threads
D) To manage peripherals
questions.explanation: A process manager is responsible for creating, scheduling, and terminating processes and threads in an operating system.

questions.question: What is the difference between a hard disk drive (HDD) and a solid-state drive (SSD)?
options: A) HDD uses flash memory, SSD uses magnetic disks
B) HDD uses flash memory, SSD uses hard disks
C) HDD uses solid-state memory, SSD uses hard disks
D) HDD uses magnetic disks, SSD uses solid-state memory
questions.explanation: A solid-state drive (SSD) uses solid-state memory, while a hard disk drive (HDD) uses magnetic disks.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a device driver in an operating system?
options: A) To manage memory allocation
B) To manage file systems
C) To manage devices and peripherals
D) To manage processes and threads
questions.explanation: A device driver is a program that manages devices and peripherals in an operating system, allowing them to communicate with the operating system.
